title: Charred Energizing Egyptian Tagine
description: A charred, pillowy Egyptian dish featuring shredded coconut, anti-inflammatory-friendly, and wrapped in a corn husk. This energizing recipe is perfect for a cozy night in.

ingredients.name: Coconut oil
ingredients.notes: Use high-quality, cold-pressed coconut oil for best flavor.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Chop the onion before cooking to ensure it cooks evenly.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic before adding to the pot.

ingredients.name: Dates
ingredients.notes: Choose fresh, pitted dates for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Shredded coconut
ingredients.notes: Shredded coconut adds a unique texture and flavor to this dish.

ingredients.name: Paprika
ingredients.notes: Smoked paprika adds a deep, smoky flavor to this recipe.

ingredients.name: Cumin
ingredients.notes: Ground cumin adds a warm, earthy flavor to this dish.

ingredients.name: Cayenne pepper
ingredients.notes: Cayenne pepper adds a spicy kick to this recipe.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use high-quality, unrefined salt for best flavor.

ingredients.name: Corn husks
ingredients.notes: Large corn husks work best for wrapping this dish.

instructions: Heat coconut oil in a large clay pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and cook until caramelized, stirring occasionally.
Add minced garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add dates, shredded coconut, paprika, cumin, cayenne pepper, and salt. Stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a large bowl and let cool slightly.
Spoon the mixture onto the center of each corn husk, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold the bottom edge of the corn husk up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the husk into a neat package.
Place the bundled tagine in a large steamer basket or Dutch oven and steam for 20-25 minutes, or until the coconut is tender and the filling is heated through.
